taskDescription: Click on the key thoracic anatomy terms in the text below.
textField:
The thoracic cage consists of 12 pairs of *ribs*, the *sternum*, and 12 *thoracic* vertebrae. *True* ribs (1-7) attach directly to the sternum via *costal* cartilages. *False* ribs (8-10) attach indirectly, and *floating* ribs (11-12) have free anterior ends. The *sternal* angle (angle of *Louis*) marks the junction of the *manubrium* and body of the sternum at the level of *T4*, corresponding to the second rib and *tracheal* *bifurcation*.

The *intercostal* *neurovascular* bundle runs in the *costal* groove in the order *VAN* (vein, artery, nerve) from superior to inferior. The *internal* *thoracic* artery (internal mammary artery) runs along the inner aspect of the anterior chest wall. A *chest* *drain* should be inserted above the upper border of a rib to avoid the neurovascular bundle.

The *diaphragm* is the primary muscle of *inspiration*, supplied by the *phrenic* nerve (C3, C4, C5). The *caval* opening is at T8, the *oesophageal* *hiatus* at T10, and the *aortic* hiatus at T12. In *flail* chest, *paradoxical* movement occurs due to multiple adjacent rib *fractures* creating a free-floating segment.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key thoracic anatomy terms accurately.
